economic_notes: Fort William First Nation holds investments in Anemki Mountain Business Trust (GBE) and Lot 251-3 Development LP (25% interest via Holdings LP). Boundary Claim Settlement Trust holds significant investment assets. Ongoing municipal property tax dispute with City of Thunder Bay over approximately 1,100 acres of returned land.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) incomplete tangible capital asset inventory - not all major asset classes have been fully capitalized as required under PS 3150; (2) property tax liabilities of $7,769,477 owing to City of Thunder Bay excluded from financial statements - First Nation disputes reserve status of approximately 1,100 acres returned in 1999. If recorded, accumulated surplus would decrease by $17,514,301. Financial statements also lack required budget comparison information. The reporting entity includes 11 consolidated subsidiaries and 2 entities accounted for under the modified equity method (Anemki Mountain Business Trust and Lot 251-3 Development LP). Large community and youth trust accounts of $103,524,298 and amounts held in trust for minors of $23,564,130 relate to specific claim settlements. Social Services and Health combined as one expense line ($5,590,029). Province is Ontario (Thunder Bay area). Restricted cash calculated as total cash minus unrestricted cash ($38,163,579 - $11,162,557 = $27,001,022).
